headline: 40 Runs, a 9–0 Massacre, and the League's Luckiest Win You'll Ever See
subheadline: Week 2 of BeerLeagueBaseball delivered a historically dominant sweep, a statistical crime against General Hospital, and a Benchwarmers win that should come with an asterisk the size of a bus.
body:
We're two weeks in and the league is already sorting itself out in the most chaotic, beautiful way possible. Some teams look like they're playing a different sport than everyone else. Some teams look like they're still reading the rulebook. And at least one team won a matchup they had absolutely no business winning. Let's get into it.

**Stat of the Week: Sugar Land Skeeters scored 40 runs in a single week — the highest run total of any team this season — powering their dominant 8–1 demolition of Hiroshima Radiation.**

Forty. Runs. That's not a fantasy baseball week, that's a Madden franchise where you turned the sliders all the way up. Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson are out here playing the game on rookie difficulty while everyone else is on All-Madden.

## At-a-Glance

| Matchup | Score | Player of the Matchup |
|---------|-------|-----------------------|
| North Correa def. Albuquerque Bombers | 7–3 | Oneil Cruz (4 HR, 10 RBI) |
| Abbotsford-Colby Twins def. Canberra Wombats | 6–4 | Yordan Alvarez (.600 OBP) |
| General Hospital def. Athletic Park Dragons | 6–4 | General Hospital pitching staff (2.10 ERA, 1.02 WHIP) |
| BaseballPro77 def. Benchwarmers | 5–4 | Corbin Carroll (only bright spot on either roster) |
| Riverside Roughnecks def. Buttville Skidmarks | 7–3 | Riverside offense (38 R, 10 HR, .365 OBP) |
| Sugar Land Skeeters def. Hiroshima Radiation | 8–1 | Drake Baldwin / Matt Olson (40 R week) |
| Wisconsin Rapids Reapers def. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | 9–0 | Ben Rice (9 R, 3 HR, 9 RBI) |

## Thriller of the Week

### BaseballPro77 def. Benchwarmers, 5–4

Look, nobody is putting either of these teams in a highlight reel. **BaseballPro77** posted a genuinely alarming 5.28 ERA and mustered exactly 2 home runs all week — 2! — while **Benchwarmers** actually out-hit them with a .343 OBP and 5 HR. And yet here we are, with BaseballPro77 escaping with a one-category win like the guy who stumbles out of a casino at 3 a.m. somehow up fifty bucks. The math doesn't add up, it never does, and that's why we watch. Both of these teams are one bad week from serious trouble — BaseballPro77 faces a real test next week and the smoke machine can only run so long.

## The Week's Defining Moment

### Wisconsin Rapids Reapers 9, Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs 0

Let me paint you a picture. One team posts a 0.84 WHIP. (For context, that's closer to "video game cheat code" than "real baseball number.") That same team drops 75 strikeouts, Ben Rice goes full superhero with 9 runs, 3 HR, and 9 RBI, and the final score is 9–0. A sweep. A perfect score. The fantasy equivalent of the 2017 Warriors going 16–1 in the playoffs — just an absolute, clinical, joyless-for-the-other-side demolition.

Now flip it around. **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** put up zero stolen bases, a league-worst zero category wins, and a .270 OBP that would make a Little League coach uncomfortable. Last week they were ranked 9th with "perceived upside." This week they look like a team that drafted exclusively in the 11th round and called it a day. Zero category wins in a 10-category format is a special kind of bad — that's not losing, that's the other team locking you out of the building.

**Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** — written off by most of the league entering Week 2 — are now a legitimate top-three contender. Ben Rice is the name to know. The Reapers have the pitching infrastructure (that WHIP! that K rate!) to carry them through rough offensive weeks, which means their ceiling just got a lot higher. The Wonderdogs, meanwhile, need to make moves. Yesterday.

## Matchup Deep-Dives

### North Correa def. Albuquerque Bombers, 7–3

Oneil Cruz went absolutely nuclear — 4 HR, 10 RBI, the kind of week that makes everyone who passed on him feel personally attacked. Ohtani chipped in, the pitching held (2.85 ERA, 4 QS), and **North Correa** cruised to a 7–3 win. **Albuquerque Bombers** weren't embarrassing — 63 Ks and 7 SB are respectable numbers — but Kyle Harrison's 1.65 ERA in a losing effort is one of the cruelest stats of the week. Bombers management has been active on waivers, which tells you everything about how they feel. North Correa, meanwhile, is a top-four team and deserves to be treated as such.

### Abbotsford-Colby Twins def. Canberra Wombats, 6–4

Yordan Alvarez with a .600 OBP is one of those stats you read twice and then look around the room to see if anyone else saw it. The **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** also tied the single-week strikeout record with 77 Ks (more on that below), added a 2.25 ERA, and handled business 6–4. **Canberra Wombats** actually out-hit them in runs (31–21) and had Altuve at .577 OBP, which is somehow also an absurd number in a losing effort. The Wombats' 7 NSVH led the league — there's talent here. The pitching (3.94 ERA) just keeps costing them. Twins are quietly elite. Wombats are quietly frustrating.

### General Hospital def. Athletic Park Dragons, 6–4

Wait, **General Hospital** won? They did. And they still somehow feel like the unlucky team (we'll get there). A 2.10 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, and .363 OBP is a genuinely excellent week and they pulled out the 6–4 W over **Athletic Park Dragons**. The Dragons have Aaron Judge and James Wood, which sounds great, until you look at that 4.30 ERA and 46 IP and realize the pitching staff is essentially a tire fire with a nice frame around it. Judge and Wood are real. The staff needs to be addressed before this team slips further down the standings.

### Riverside Roughnecks def. Buttville Skidmarks, 7–3

The most complete offensive performance outside of Sugar Land this week. **Riverside Roughnecks** dropped 38 R, 10 HR, and a .365 OBP while keeping the ERA at 2.42. They look nothing like the sketchy pretenders from Week 1's narrative — this team is for real. **Buttville Skidmarks** had decent pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS) and it didn't matter because the offense couldn't keep up. That's been the story two weeks running. Until Buttville's bats wake up, this team is going to keep losing the close ones.

### Sugar Land Skeeters def. Hiroshima Radiation, 8–1

**Sugar Land Skeeters** scored 40 runs. I'll just leave that there. **Hiroshima Radiation** — who genuinely looked like a contender in Week 1 — posted only 43 Ks, a 1 category win, and got run out of the gym. What happened? Their pitching held up okay (3.29 ERA, 3 QS) but everything else went sideways. One week of results doesn't define a season, but one category win in Week 2 is a red flag. The Skeeters are the class of the league right now. Full stop.

### Wisconsin Rapids Reapers def. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs, 9–0

Already covered in The Week's Defining Moment, but let me add one thing: **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** had a 0.84 WHIP. The league average is probably hovering around 1.25. A 0.84 is not a number that should exist in a competitive fantasy league. It's the kind of stat that makes you wonder if the Reapers accidentally imported a team from a different universe.

## Lucky/Unlucky Team of the Week

**🍀 Luckiest Team: Benchwarmers**

A 4.89 ERA. 42 strikeouts. 46 innings pitched. The second-highest luck delta in the entire league (+0.58). And somehow, inexplicably, a 5–4 win. The **Benchwarmers** didn't earn this one — they found it in the couch cushions. Corbin Carroll is a legitimate bright spot, but the rest of this roster is running on vibes and favorable scheduling. This is the fantasy equivalent of going 3-for-3 on coin flips. Eventually the coin lands the other way.

**😤 Unluckiest Team: General Hospital**

This is legitimately criminal. **General Hospital** posted a 2.10 ERA. A 1.02 WHIP. A .363 OBP. Thirty runs scored. They won their matchup 6–4 — but their luck delta of -0.52 is tied for the worst in the league, meaning those elite numbers were almost entirely wasted. The talent on this roster is real. The schedule has not been their friend. When the luck normalizes — and it will — General Hospital is going to make some noise.

## League Records Watch

**North Correa** tied the single-week RBI record with 38 RBI, matching the previous mark — set by North Correa themselves in Week 2. (Sure, it's technically a tie, but setting the record and then immediately matching your own record is a very specific kind of power move I respect.) No new record was broken.

**Abbotsford-Colby Twins** tied the single-week strikeout record with 77 K, matching the previous mark of 77 — also set by Abbotsford-Colby Twins in Week 2. Same situation, same team, same week. These two franchises are so dominant in their categories they're literally only competing against themselves. Everyone else is just trying to keep up.

## Manager Spotlight: Athletic Park Dragons

Here's the thing about the **Athletic Park Dragons**: the bones are good. Aaron Judge is Aaron Judge. James Wood has All-Star upside. Their offense posted 28 runs and 7 HR this week against a solid General Hospital squad — that's not nothing. They currently sit 9th in the power rankings, which is probably about right, but the gap between where they are and where they *could* be is entirely the pitching staff's fault.

A 4.30 ERA, 46 IP, and 2 quality starts is not a foundation you can win with consistently. The Dragons' manager has the hitters to compete with anyone on a given week — the Judge/Wood combo is legitimately one of the most exciting offensive pairings in the league — but until the rotation gets fixed, every week is going to feel like trying to win a tug-of-war with one hand tied behind your back. The playoff outlook isn't dire yet. But if the pitching situation doesn't improve by Week 4 or 5, we're going to start having a different conversation about this team's ceiling.

## Trade Value Report

**Oneil Cruz (North Correa)** — 4 HR, 10 RBI in a single week, following a strong Week 1. The power is undeniable and the upside here is massive. **VERDICT: BUY** — the price just went up but Cruz is a legitimate difference-maker in HR and RBI categories when he's locked in, and right now he looks very locked in.

**Ben Rice (Wisconsin Rapids Reapers)** — 9 R, 3 HR, 9 RBI. Came out of nowhere on the national fantasy radar and is now a household name in this league. **VERDICT: BUY** — the Reapers' pitching infrastructure means Rice's offensive contributions matter, and a two-week hot streak on a 9–0 team is not a coincidence.

**Hiroshima Radiation's Pitching Staff** — 43 Ks, 1 category win, a shocking reversal from Week 1. **VERDICT: SELL** (on the margins) — the ERA was acceptable (3.29) but the strikeout and overall category performance is concerning. If you're holding Radiation pitchers on a team that needs Ks, explore the market.

## Power Rankings

1. **Sugar Land Skeeters** | Back-to-back elite weeks, 40 R this week is a season record, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both starred — this roster is the class of the league | 🔺
2. **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** | 9–0 sweep with a 0.84 WHIP and 75 Ks is historically dominant; Ben Rice is a must-watch breakout — this team is ascending fast | 🔺
3. **Riverside Roughnecks** | 7–3 win with 38 R, 10 HR, and a 2.42 ERA — this is the most complete performance of their young season and they look nothing like last week's pretenders | 🔺
4. **North Correa** | Oneil Cruz went nuclear with 4 HR and 10 RBI, Ohtani chipped in — 7–3 result with elite power and solid pitching makes this a legitimate contender | 🔺
5. **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP is absurd and 77 Ks anchors elite pitching; the 6–4 win was workmanlike but this roster has genuine top-tier upside | ➡️
6. **General Hospital** | A 2.10 ERA and .363 OBP losing 6–4 is a crime; the talent is real and the luck will turn — don't sleep on this team despite the ugly result | 🔻
7. **BaseballPro77** | Escaped with a 5–4 squeaker on the back of terrible ERA and only 2 HR — this team is living dangerously, but two weeks of wins is two weeks of wins | 🔻
8. **Hiroshima Radiation** | Got destroyed 8–1 in a shocking reversal after looking strong; only 43 Ks and 1 category win is an organizational crisis that needs urgent attention | 🔻
9. **Athletic Park Dragons** | Aaron Judge and James Wood are elite pieces but the pitching (4.30 ERA, 46 IP, 2 QS) is genuinely holding this team back from its ceiling | 🔻
10. **Buttville Skidmarks** | Lost 7–3 despite decent pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS) — the offense simply isn't reliable enough to compete with top teams most weeks | ➡️
11. **Canberra Wombats** | Lost 6–4 with real offensive contributors in Altuve (.577 OBP), Walker, and Henderson — they're not as bad as their record suggests but pitching keeps killing them | 🔺
12. **Albuquerque Bombers** | Kyle Harrison was genuinely brilliant (1.65 ERA, 26 K) but lost 7–3 anyway; heavy waiver activity suggests the manager knows change is needed | ➡️
13. **Benchwarmers** | A 5–4 win built on smoke and mirrors — 4.89 ERA, 42 Ks, and a historically lucky result; Corbin Carroll is a bright spot on an otherwise fragile roster | ➡️
14. **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero
